job.title: Business Development Manager – IT Staffing Services (34025)
job.description:
Title: Business Development Manager – IT Staffing Services.

About us: Myticas Consulting Group of Companies. Myticas specializes in IT Staffing and Recruitment Services with over 50 years of combined experience in the IT Staffing industry. We serve clients in all industries in the United States and Canada with offices in Ottawa, Chicago, Atlanta, Cleveland, and Grand Rapids. 

About the position: The Sr. Business Development professional will sit in our Chicago, Illinois office This position is a hybrid role and requires some travel. This position is responsible for Business Development in this area as a priority, but able to develop business in OH, PA, WV, IN, IL, etc. This position comes with a competitive salary, commission, bonus potential, medical, dental, vision, 401K, and PTO. This professional would have full access to training, access to and support from the marketing department and technical tools in support of their success. 

Job Duties and Responsibilities:

  • Initiate contact, develop relationships, and conduct business development activities with clients and professionals; networking and connecting with IT professionals, executives, managers, and other professionals and resources at potential, new, and existing clients.
  • Research and qualify new clients, conducting business development activities and negotiating terms of service and agreements with new clients.
  • Provided expert level staffing support to clients, hiring managers, and professionals. Qualifying technical position requirements and coordinating with technical recruiters to fulfill staffing needs for new and existing clients.
  • Represent and market the  Myticas Consulting Group brands.
  • Participate, network at, support, and sponsor events involving the technical community.
  • Sell via telephone, face-to-face and videoconferencing.
  • Utilize Bullhorn CRM/Application tracking system for client and manager outreach and keep CRM documentation, agreements, resources, and paperwork up to date.

Required Experience:

  • 3-5 years’ demonstrated record of success selling staffing services, preferably with experience in the IT environment.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
  • Outstanding customer service, integrity, and interpersonal skills.  
  • Energetic, enthusiastic, self-motivated, collaborative.
  • Passion for the staffing industry, excellent work ethic, and strong desire to succeed.

job.title: Sr Marketing Data Scientist (MMM) (33918)
job.description:
Myticas direct client is seeking Marketing Data Scientist Manager for a 6 months+ contract-to-hire opportunity.

Location: 100% REMOTE
Duration: 6 Months Contract to Hire

Title:

  • Marketing Data Science Manager

About the Role

We are seeking a Marketing Data Science Manager who can bridge the gap between data modeling and business impact. This role will focus on leveraging data science models and analytical frameworks to generate actionable marketing insights, optimize campaign performance, and guide strategic decisions.
The ideal candidate combines strong analytical and technical skills with business acumen and communication ability to translate data into meaningful narratives for stakeholders.

Requirements

  • Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Statistics, Economics, Operation Research, Computer Science, Industrial Engineering, Mathematics, Marketing Analytics etc.
  • Analytical and technical skills:
    • Proficiency (10+ years) in analyzing complex data and models, derive and deliver actionable insights to provide data driven marketing strategy to stakeholders. Strong ability to translate model outputs into business insights and marketing recommendations.
    • Proficiency (7+ years) in statistical software such as SQL, R, Python, PBI for data wrangling and modeling.
    • Familiarity (5+ years) with AWS Sagemaker and Lambda functions. Terraform experience will be preferred.
    • Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
  • Experience (required):
    • At least 10 years hands on experience in machine learning, marketing analytics, statistical modeling, marketing mix modeling.
    • At least 10 years experience with various statistical techniques such as regression analysis, time series analysis, and predictive modeling, optimization etc.
    • Familiarity (5+ years) with marketing research methodologies and tools.
  • Experience (good to have):
    • AI and multi-touch attribution model experience
    • Familiarity with ad platforms (Google Ads, DV360, etc.)
    • Experience in A/B testing or experimentation frameworks
  • Marketing Knowledge:
    • Deep understanding of marketing concepts, including advertising, distribution, and campaign strategies.
    • Familiarity with different marketing channels and their impact on business performance.
    • Knowledge of industry trends and best practices in marketing analytics.
  • Business Acumen:
    • Understanding of business objectives and how marketing analytics can support decision-making and drive business growth.
    • Ability to align marketing strategies with broader organizational goals and objectives.
  • Communication Skills:
    • Ability to communicate complex analytical findings to non-technical stakeholders in a clear and concise manner.
    • Collaborative approach to working with cross-functional teams, including media, creative, product, and finance.

Skills

  • Proficient in machine learning model and statistical model development, simulation and optimization and deployment in AWS
  • Proficient in SQL, Python, R for data integration and modeling projects
  • Advanced in AWS Sagemaker, EC2, S3, Terraform, Bitbucket, etc for deployment projects
  • Advanced in PBI, Excel, streamlit for analytics and visualization projects
  • Experience in Terraform, EC2, MMM, optimizer application will be a big plus

Key Responsibilities:

  • Develop and deploy predictive and statistical models to evaluate marketing performance (e.g., attribution, media mix modeling, churn, LTV, segmentation, optimization).
  • Partner with marketing, product team to translate modeling results into clear business insights & recommendations that drive growth and ROI and communicate implications to non-technical stakeholders.
  • Present findings to senior stakeholders through clear storytelling and visualization.
  • Visualize and present analytical results using tools like Power BI, Excel, etc.
  • Build and maintain scalable analytics pipelines using Python/R, SQL, and possibly cloud data platforms (GCP, AWS, Azure).
  • Partner with marketing operations to operationalize insights (e.g., audience targeting, spend optimization).
  • Collaborate with data engineers and analysts to improve data accessibility and accuracy.
  • Support ongoing model performance monitoring and iterative improvements.
  • Support MMM and app maintenance and development for simulation and optimization and further development. Push changes to production using AWS Sagemaker, EC2, Terraform, Bitbucket, etc.
  • Assist with ad-hoc data requests, data exploration and building business knowledge models. Build intelligent dashboards if needed using SQL, PBI. 
  • Conduct cross-functional collaboration, stakeholder meetings and provide documentations and internal trainings.
  • Lead projects, manage timelines, and ensure deliverables meet the business objectives and are delivered on time.
  • Perform other duties as assigned or apparent.
  • Work with marketing and product teams to design experiments, measure campaign ROI, and inform strategy.

What You’ll Gain

  • Opportunity to help shape the organization’s marketing data science and insights function.
  • Direct influence on marketing strategy and customer growth initiatives.
  • Exposure to cutting-edge analytics and experimentation techniques in a data-driven culture.
  • Path to full-time employment with strong career growth opportunities.

job.title: Industrial Cybersecurity Consultant (34053)
job.description:
Industrial Cybersecurity Consultant
STSI is looking for a Cyber Security Consultant who is detail oriented with a willingness to utilize their investigative mind and dig into remediation issues, conduct security audits, and implement change plans in IT & OT environments. Ideal candidates have experience assessing security and PLC controls with experience conducting security audits, maintaining up to date understanding of guidance from the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), and have a firm understanding and experience with Allen Bradley PLC and Modbus PLC universal communication protocol.

Type: Full-time Contract - W2 ONLY, accepting candidate that are a US Citizen (nonnegotiable)
Compensation: $ 50.00 USD to 60.00 USD
# of Positions: 3-5
Location: Richmond, VA and Glen Allen, VA
Travel: 80% - 100% - accepting only local candidates in the state of Virginia who are able to drive/go to different locations/offices in VA. Consultants will use their own vehicle and must have a valid driver's license.
Expense: Miles, Hotel (when required), food are reimbursed expenses.
Schedule: Daylight, Monday through Friday.
Reports to: Director of Network & Endpoint Security, Project Manager & Delivery Lead

Position Summary:
The Cybersecurity Consultant will independently execute significant portions of projects addressing Information Technology (IT) and Industrial Control System security. The Cybersecurity Consultant supports the execution of projects consisting of network penetration testing, web application security testing, cybersecurity vulnerability assessments, secure system design and integration, and/or development of cybersecurity programs at client sites across the U.S and Canada utilizing the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) Cybersecurity Framework (CSF), NIST Risk Management Framework (RMF), and other industry or data specific compliance frameworks and regulations. Execute the planning, design, development and implementation of technical controls, procedures and policy associated with cybersecurity compliance and/or regulatory standards.


Responsibilities:
  • Maintain highest level of integrity, protecting the confidentiality and security of all clients and project information.
  • Identify and diagnose operational issues and implement design alterations to address these issues.
  • Conduct network penetration tests and vulnerability assessments of IT and Operational Technology (OT) networks, for both compliance and security purposes.
  • Perform detailed, post event analysis of unusual events, and direct needed procedure or process changes in response.
  • Pursue, obtain, and maintain industry recognized IT certifications related to cybersecurity such as ethical hacking, network engineering, Industrial Control System (ICS), Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA), risk management, and others as necessary.
  • Resolve technical issues, analyze implications to the client's business, and be able to communicate them with applicable stakeholders within the business.
  • Develop policies & procedures for secure process control network design, technical and design recommendations for the implementation of firewalls and other network security and compliance controls.
  • Compiles technical documentation of network traffic as well as firewall services/solutions including explanations and diagrams.
  • Work collaboratively with other groups and divisions inside the company.
  • Performs other duties as assigned.
  • Comply with all policies and standards.
  • Performs other duties as assigned.

Required Experience:
  • Bachelor’s degree in Cybersecurity, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related technical field and min 3 years of related/relevant experience is Required.
  • Advanced knowledge of security principles and firm knowledge of cybersecurity technologies, as well as industry-recognized certifications.
  • Experience with cybersecurity vulnerability assessments, penetration tests, and the tools/techniques involved in both.
  • Experience in the capabilities and/or configuration of cybersecurity controls, specifically those relating to firewalls, access control, authentication, anti-virus/anti-malware, patching, and logging.
  • Advanced knowledge of control systems utilized by utilities, manufacturing, oil and gas, transportation, smart buildings, and cities.
  • Strong written and oral communication skills.
  • Strong analytical and critical thinking skills.
  • Ability to operate under pressure and under tight deadlines, to operate in on-site industrial, corporate, and government work.
  • Demonstrate capability to make sound decisions based on good security practices and principles.
  • Demonstrate an understanding of business principles and operational security practices specific to engineering and/or security consulting.
  • Knowledge and/or experience with corporate policies and procedures.
  • Strong technical writing skills. Knowledge and experience with modern and legacy computer networking and telecommunications.
  • Experience with physical cabling for network communications and control system Input/Output.
  • Ability to obtain and maintain access to current and future client sites.

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other legally protected status. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to any of these characteristics.

job.description:

job.title: Sr. Electrical Engineer (34506)
job.description:
Location: Akron, OH (Hybrid – 3 days in office)
Job Type: Full-Time, Direct Hire
Schedule: Hybrid (on-site collaboration required)
Compensation: $130,000–$150,000 base salary
(Exempt/salaried role; additional compensation eligibility may apply for workload demands beyond standard hours)
 

Position Summary

The Senior Electrical Engineer is responsible for leading and executing electrical engineering design efforts for industrial projects. This role involves gathering and analyzing technical data, developing electrical designs, and supporting projects through construction and commissioning phases.

Typical project work includes power distribution, lighting systems, electrical one-line diagrams, load studies, protective relaying, arc flash studies, hazardous area classifications, and equipment specifications. The position requires periodic visits to client facilities and may include hands-on field support.

Key Responsibilities

  • Perform electrical engineering calculations and develop detailed design packages

  • Lead and coordinate designers and engineers within a project team

  • Prepare electrical deliverables including:

    • Power and lighting systems

    • Electrical one-line drawings

    • Electrical load studies

    • Protective relaying and short-circuit studies

    • Arc flash studies

    • Area classifications

    • Control schematics and installation details

  • Specify electrical equipment such as:

    • Switchgear, transformers, MCCs, motors, UPS systems

    • Grounding and bonding systems

  • Utilize engineering and design software to complete assigned tasks efficiently

  • Serve as discipline lead on electrical-only projects by:

    • Estimating engineering hours

    • Defining scope of work

    • Recommending staffing needs

    • Reviewing and checking drawings

  • Support field activities including:

    • Installation reviews

    • Troubleshooting and problem-solving

    • Coordination with contractors and vendors

  • Maintain compliance with company policies, procedures, safety standards, and applicable codes

  • Promote safe work practices and identify potentially unsafe conditions

  • Perform additional duties as assigned

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering from an ABET-accredited university

  • Professional Engineer (PE) license or an EIT who has met the requirements to take the PE and have at hand in a few month

  • Strong field experience in industrial environments

  • 12–30 years of experience in one or more of the following industries:

    • Chemicals

    • Petrochemicals

    • Polymers

    • Utilities

    • Energy

    • Manufacturing

  • Extensive experience with:

    • Hazardous Area Classifications (process industries)

    • Electrical power distribution systems

    • Protective relaying and short-circuit analysis

    • Arc flash analysis

  • Working knowledge of:

    • NEC / NFPA 70

    • NFPA 30

    • Applicable building and electrical codes

  • Proficiency in:

    • Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)

    • AutoCAD (3D preferred)

    • Revit

    • EasyPower or similar electrical modeling software

  • Experience interpreting and developing:

    • P&IDs

    • PFDs

    • Loop diagrams

    • Conduit, cable, and equipment schedules

job.title: Senior Project Manager (34606)
job.description:
Senior Project Manager
Charlotte, NC (On-site)

Benefits: Access to client benefits package 
Pay Range: $80.00 - $85.96 

Responsibilities:
  • Experience managing mid-large capital power projects, with specific experience in combined cycle power plants. Project experience should involve management of multiple engineering disciplines and vendor management from early phases of project through start-up and commissioning.
  • Monitoring, reporting, and controlling project costs and schedule through all phases of the Project.
  • Execute of all aspects of the project including managing the staff, providing vision and strategic leadership, monitoring project schedules and budgets, evaluating project productivity and quality and directing the complete technical operations of the project.
  • Experience in successfully managing and executing work with overseas High Value Engineering (HVE) offices.
  • Develop and implement administrative, technical and corporate procedures and policies to ensure the successful operation of the project on a profitable basis.
  • Direct and manage the activities of Project personnel and support the team in the planning, scheduling, design quality and execution of projects.
  • Assume full responsibility for the successful execution of each assigned project for schedule compliance, budget compliance, and scope and quality of deliverables.
  • Build relationships and coordinate with the client and project personnel.
  • Ensure the client's input and job requirements are adequately addressed and implemented consistently with the contractual requirements.
  • Organize and supervise the implementation of project management systems and corporate quality systems.
  • Maintain a documentation file, accurately describing all commercial, technical and financial correspondence.
  • Prepare and evaluate monthly progress reports, describing the schedule, manpower expenditures and work scope for accuracy, completeness, and timeliness.
  • Ensure invoices are processed timely and accurately per contract agreements. Act as a liaison between the client and the accounting department on any outstanding invoices.
  • Champion Management of Change by identifying and documenting ECNs and processing in a timely fashion where additional work is required.
  • Develop new business opportunities as a by-product of relationship with clients. Participate in business development meetings.
  • Plan and identify upcoming tasks, potential problem areas, technical information, staffing priorities and identify solutions where necessary.
  • Responsible to maintain proper staff levels to meet project requirements. Actively recruit and train personnel for project as necessary.
  • Develop, coach and track employee skill level in technical and work management areas.
  • Develops project proposals, proposal scopes of work, and budgets.
  • Performs field walk downs and data collection.
  • Position necessitates field activities, some climbing, and occasional travel.

Qualifications:
  • Bachelor’s degree in engineering.
  • 15 to 20 years of experience on Power projects, specifically combined cycle.
  • At least 8-10 years as a Project Manager.
  • Experienced in managing a portfolio of projects ranging from small to mid-cap projects, as well as mid-large cap standalone projects of $100MM TIC and up.
  • Strong communication and inter-personal skills are required to provide effective intra and inter-discipline coordination on projects, as well as effective communication with clients and vendors.
  • Must be organized, self-directed, able to prioritize and multi-task, work in a team environment and take direction from others.
  • Willingness to learn and work on a variety of tasks within multiple industries.
  • Willingness to travel and work in office and field environments.
  • Full and ongoing compliance with and adherence to all vaccination requirements, as may be established by the company from time to time or by or for a particular job site.



We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other legally protected status. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to any of these characteristics.

job.title: Mechanic (34598)
job.description:
Mechanic 
The Villages, FL (On-site)

Benefits: Standard Medical, Dental, 401(k)
Pay Range:  $22.00 /hr. - $24.00 /hr.         


Summary: Maintain, diagnose, and repair hydraulic, electrical, and powertrain systems on heavy-duty waste vehicles to ensure DOT compliance and operational safety.

Responsibilities:
  • Preventive Maintenance (PM): Performing oil changes, inspections, and safety checks on diesel engines, suspension, and brake systems.
  • System Diagnostics: Troubleshooting complex hydraulic and electrical systems specific to sanitation equipment (compactors, lift arms).
  • Component Repair: Replacing hydraulic cylinders, hoses, diesel engines, and transmission parts.
  • Compliance: Ensuring all vehicles meet Department of Transportation (DOT) and OSHA safety standards.

Requirements:
  • Experience: Minimum 3 years of experience in heavy-duty truck maintenance.
  • Licensure: CDL Class B is required.
  • Skills: Proficiency in hydraulic systems, diagnostic software, and electrical systems.

job.title: SAP Technical Analyst (34615)
job.description:
Clover Consulting has a direct client in Grand Rapids, MI in need of an SAP Technical Analyst for a contract or contract-to-hire opportunity.  This positon will require on-site work in Grand Rapids, MI.  Candidates local or willing to relocate should apply.  

TITLE:  SAP S/4 Technical Analyst
TERM: 10+ Months Contract or Contract-to-Hire
TYPE:  W2 or C2C
RATE:  Negotiable
LOCATION:  Grand Rapids, MI - ON-SITE work required
Candidates willing to relocate will be considered

Technical Analyst
We are looking for a technical analyst with primarily SAP S/4 experience to contribute to the objectives of our global team and its ongoing projects. In this role, you will apply Agile methodologies to facilitate the efficient delivery of robust SAP S/4 technical solutions, that advance cross-functional business strategies and initiatives.
 
The team you will be part of    
 
We are a global team managing the migration from SAP ECC to S/4 to support manufacturing, distribution, and logistics. Our versatile members ensure smooth transition and ongoing ECC technical support, working collaboratively to achieve team objectives and align with company goals.
 
Primary Responsibilities
 
As a technical analyst, your responsibilities will include:
·Interpreting functional specifications and translating them into implementable and supportable technical solutions
·Developing technical specifications utilizing standardized templates
·Creating and maintaining comprehensive technical documentation
·Conducting research on S/4 platform best practices to address business process requirements
·Collaborating with technical developers to ensure deliverables meet business requirements and expectations
·Unit testing developed solutions
·Troubleshooting business and technical processes on platforms such as SAP ECC, S/4, and .Net
·Applying minor ABAP code enhancements and SAP configuration
·Applying Agile methodologies and principles throughout the entire development lifecycle
·Maintaining up-to-date knowledge of emerging technologies and proactively sharing innovative ideas for process improvement
·Providing support for end-user requests or issues through the incident reporting tool, ServiceNow
 
Knowledge and Skills
·Experience in SAP S/4
·Experience in SAP S/4 modules PPDS, EWM, and/or TM
·Experience in maintaining SAP system configuration (SPRO)
·Experience in SAP ECC a plus
·Experience with Agile methodologies and concepts
·Experience in debugging transactions, ABAP programs, and function modules
·Experience in writing ABAP code a plus
·Experience with Microsoft Office Suite, including Word, Excel, Visio, and PowerPoint
·Knowledgeable in all aspects of ABAP development, including reports, enhancements, interfaces, function modules, workflows, and classes
·Knowledgeable in Fiori, SAPUI5, CDS views, OData technologies, APIs, Cloud Platform, HANA DB, and implementing or extending standard SAP Fiori applications
·Knowledgeable in BADIs, enhancement points, BAPIs, IDOCs, and Web Services
·Knowledgeable in Discreet Manufacturing a plus
·Knowledgeable in Make to Order (MTO) a plus
·Knowledgeable in manufacturing BOMs and Routings a plus
·Knowledgeable with Azure DevOps (ADO) a plus
 
Who you are
 
·Build strong relationships and communicate clearly and effectively with diverse team members
·Demonstrate a strong work ethic, share knowledge, achieve goals, and meet deadlines
·Apply solid problem-solving and analytical abilities
·Adaptable, as team members must work in modules/areas of S/4 and ECC that they are not familiar with
·Facilitate data-driven discussions and decisions
·Support projects that enhance business capabilities
·Tackle complex system challenges with intuitive thinking
·Stay updated on system methods, emerging technologies, and best practices
·Focus on serving the team, customers, IT, and Steelcase
·Collaborate with cross-functional, global teams
·Lead and teach others empathetically

job.title: Piping AutoCAD Plant 3D Designer (34594)
job.description:
Piping Designer
Location: Mobile, AL or Kansas City, MO or Appleton, WI 
Benefits: Standard Medical, Dental, Vision
Hourly Range: $50.00-$70.00/hr

Client provides full-service engineering, procurement and construction management services to industrial process manufacturers that operate in pulp & paper, chemical, power & delivery, heavy industrial, consumer product and food & beverage market. We are in a significant growth mode and it’s an exciting time to be joining our business!  

The piping designer will be responsible for creating detailed designs and layouts for piping systems, ensuring accuracy, efficiency, and compliance with industry standards. 
  • CAD Design of basic equipment layouts, piping routings that are consistent with codes, standards, and client requirements. 
  • Coordinate work of other lead designers assigned to the task. 
  • Ensure that discipline leads have the correct project standards and checklists. 
  • Maintain and update the overall 3D model as required. 
  • Prepare engineering drawings and documentation. 
  • Participate in meetings with clients as requested by Project Manager. 
 
YOUR IDEAL BACKGROUND: 
The successful candidate should have an entrepreneurial spirit, and be excited by opportunities to add value to our clients engineering expertise. Strong communication skills, collaboration and courage to challenge the status quo are cornerstones.
  • +5 years of experience working within piping engineer and/or design.  Prior engineering services experience preferred
  • Proficiency in AutoCAD Plant 3D for seamless integration with existing design workflows. 
  • Ability to read client Piping Specifications
  • Experience walking down P&ID's, completing field measurements, Isometric drawings preferred
  • Ability to lead a small team to track deliverables
  • Associates Degree in Drafting or Design  
  • On-hands experience in developing equipment, overall facility layouts, preparation of overall 3D piping model, preparation of orthographic drawings, isometrics, bill of materials, and selection and design of piping supports

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other legally protected status. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to any of these characteristics.

job.title: 100% Remote - Senior Salesforce Developer, ONLY W2 (34643)
job.description:
Myticas's direct client, based in Chicago, IL is currently seeking a Senior Salesforce Developer for a 100% Remote contract position.

​**NOTE: ONLY, US Citizens and W2 Contract**

Top 3 competencies required for this position:
  1. Must have experience implementing and deploying custom solutions using Salesforce Apex programming language in an enterprise environment.
  2. Must have experience in Salesforce Integration patterns.
  3. Must have experience in Salesforce Flow optimization.

Job Overview
We are seeking a skilled Salesforce Developer to join our team. The ideal candidate will have strong expertise in Apex programming and a deep understanding of the Salesforce platform. This role is responsible for designing, developing, testing, and deploying Salesforce solutions that meet our business requirements.

Responsibilities:
  • Develop custom solutions using Salesforce Apex programming language.
  • Create and maintain triggers, batch processes, and integrations.
  • Design and implement technical solutions based on business requirements.
  • Troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues.
  • Collaborate with administrators, business analysts, and other developers.
  • Develop and maintain technical documentation.
  • Participate in code reviews to ensure quality and maintainability.
  • Stay updated with Salesforce releases and best practices.

Required Qualifications:
  • 3+ years of experience as a Salesforce Developer.
  • Strong proficiency in Apex programming (Triggers, Controllers, Batch, Async).
  • Expertise in Lightning Component Framework.
  • Expert knowledge of Salesforce triggers and best practices for their implementation.
  • Hands-on experience with Salesforce Flow.
  • Solid understanding of Salesforce administration fundamentals.
  • Experience with Salesforce data model, security model, and configuration.
  • Knowledge of Salesforce Governor Limits and how to work within them.
  • Experience with version control systems (Git preferred).
  • Salesforce Platform Developer I certification (Platform Developer II preferred).


Preferred Qualifications:
  • Experience with MuleSoft for integration projects.
  • Knowledge of Lightning Web Components (LWC).
  • Experience with Salesforce CPQ or other Salesforce clouds.
  • Experience with Salesforce DX and modern development workflows.
  • Additional Salesforce certifications (Admin, App Builder, etc.).
  • Experience with JavaScript, HTML, and CSS.
  • Agile development methodology experience.
  • Experience with Sales and Marketing Cloud is a plus.


Skills & Attributes:
  • Strong problem-solving abilities and analytical thinking.
  • Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal.
  • Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
  • Detail-oriented with a focus on quality.
  • Good time management and ability to prioritize tasks.


Education:
  • Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related field (or equivalent work experience).


Top 3 soft skills:
  • Collaboration/Teamwork
  • Adaptability (adjusts to changes in priorities)
  • Critical Thinking (applies objective analysis and reasoning)

job.title: Art Director (34583)
job.description:
Job Title: Art Director
Contract: W2– Candidates must be authorized to work in the U.S. on a W2 basis. We cannot accept candidates requiring sponsorship or those from C2C vendors.
Duration: 6 months with possible multi-year extension/s
Pay: Negotiable
Hours: Part-Time, 20 hours/week - Flexible work time
Location: ON-SITE, Grand Rapids, MI; Considering local candidates only


We are seeking a Part-Time Art Director to play a critical role in driving brand continuity and creative excellence across the entire organization. This individual will collaborate with internal teams and external agency partners to ensure all creative work aligns with brand standards and delivers a cohesive customer experience.
The Art Director will flex across the full creative development process—from shaping brand strategy to overseeing final delivery tactics. This role will touch every aspect of the business, including product brands, go-to-market campaigns, education and training, sales, and recognition programs. By connecting the dots across these areas, you will help improve the return on creative investment and amplify the impact of our creative efforts across brands and channels.

Key Responsibilities

• Brand Stewardship: Maintain and enforce brand standards across all creative outputs, ensuring consistency and continuity throughout the organization.
• Creative Development: Contribute to ideation, concept development, and execution of campaigns, assets, and experiences that span multiple touchpoints.
• Cross-Organizational Collaboration: Work closely with teams across product marketing, education, sales, and recognition to deliver integrated creative solutions.
• Agency Partnership: Collaborate with external agencies to ensure alignment with brand strategy and creative objectives.
• Customer Experience Alignment: Help connect creative efforts across channels to create a seamless and impactful customer journey.
• Strategic Input: Provide creative insights during brand and campaign strategy discussions, translating them into compelling visual executions.
• Performance Impact: Drive creative solutions that improve engagement, strengthen brand equity, and maximize ROI on creative investments.

Qualifications

• Experience: 7–10 years in marketing or communications, preferably within an internal corporate creative team.
• Creative Expertise: Deep understanding of brand systems, visual identity, and multi-channel creative execution.
• Technical Skills: Proficiency in Adobe Creative Cloud software (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign, Premiere), Figma, and presentation tools (PowerPoint, Keynote).
• Ability to work across the creative process—from strategy to production.
• Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with experience influencing cross-functional teams.
• Experience managing external creative partners is a plus.

What We Offer

• Flexible part-time schedule.
• Internal creative project managers will coordinate schedules, rounds of review, and proof timing.
• 20-25 hours per week.
• Opportunity to work on diverse brands and projects across the organization.
• Collaborative and creative work environment.

job.title: Sr Systems Engineer (W2 and Local to Chicago) (34563)
job.description:
Myticas's direct client, based in Chicago, IL, is currently seeking a Software Engineer – III for a 1 Year On-site contract position.

NOTE: Looking for W2 candidates.

Top Required Hard Skills:

• Subject matter expertise in areas such as:

o PKI, LDAP, routers, firewalls, networking.

o Middleware solutions (e.g., Sterling Connect:Direct, IBM MQ, SecureTransport Server).

o Proxy solutions (Sterling Secure Proxy, Tivoli Access Manager) and client-side technologies (Axway SecureClient).

•  Strong understanding of operating systems (Windows, Unix/Linux, Solaris).

•  Ability to design and lead customer validation tests, develop use cases, and create detailed testing plans aligned with business and technical requirements.

Top Required Soft Skills:

• Proven ability to lead technical and non-technical teams without direct authority.

• Strong skills in:

o Project management methodologies.

o Coordinating resources for testing and operational activities.

o Communicating technical concepts in business terms to executives and stakeholders.

•  Demonstrated capability to drive process improvements, manage complex customer problems, and lead customer calls for critical issues.

Job Description:
The Senior Systems Engineer Contractor leads the technical design and validation of client's solutions supporting national financial services and external organizations. This role manages technical labs, ensuring environments(hardware and software) remain current, secure, and ready for testing, validation, and certification activities. The position requires advanced technical expertise, strong leadership in cross-functional collaboration, and the ability to drive process improvements while maintaining compliance with client security standards. Ability to quickly triage major issues within application flows is vital.

PRINCIPAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:
• Technical Design & Validation
o Lead design and validation of Fclient's solutions.
o Identify gaps between current and future states; develop solutions to close those gaps.
o Design and execute customer validation tests, including detailed test plans and use cases aligned with security and compliance standards.
• Lab Management
o Oversee technical labs to maintain currency with evolving technologies.
o Ensure readiness for testing, validation, and certification activities.
• Testing & Analysis
o Coordinate technical and end-user resources to execute tests and analyze results.
o Document findings, communicate issues, and provide timelines to stakeholders.
o Assess labor and technical skill sets required for testing activities.
• Operational Support
o Implement customer-side monitoring tools.
o Lead operational outage events and manage complex customer problem resolution.
o Drive process improvement efforts for client channels.
• Performance & Standards
o Analyze client solutions for performance and cost optimization.
o Develop and maintain client;s standards; monitor SLA targets and ensure alignment with strategic objectives.

REQUIRED EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE:
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, MIS, or related field (graduate degree preferred).
  • 5–7+ years of direct technical work experience, including 2+ years in leadership roles within complex organizatio
  • Proficiency in PKI, LDAP, networking, routers, firewalls, middleware (Sterling Connect:Direct, IBM MQ, SecureTransport), proxy solutions (Sterling Secure Proxy, Tivoli Access Manager), and client-side technologies (Axway SecureClient).
  • Familiarity with multiple operating systems (Windows, Unix/Linux, Solaris).

Other Required Skills:
  • Advanced knowledge of IT testing methodologies, infrastructure and integration testing.
  • Strong understanding of project management methodologies.
  • Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making abilities.
  • Strong background in production systems 24/7 x 365 support
  • Ability to influence and lead cross-functional teams without direct authority.
  • Exceptional written, verbal, and presentation skills; ability to communicate technical concepts to non-technical audiences.

job.title: Structural Revit Designer (34645)
job.description:
Structural Revit Designer
Location: Cincinnati, OH
$110k - $120k

About the Role

A growing engineering consultancy is seeking a skilled Structural Designer to join its expanding team. The ideal candidate will bring strong technical design experience using Revit and AutoCAD, with a focus on industrial and manufacturing facilities. This role will support multi-discipline project teams to deliver high-quality engineering design packages from concept through construction.

What You’ll Do

  • Develop structural design models and drawings in Revit and AutoCAD for steel, concrete, and foundation systems.

  • Support engineers in preparing detailed design packages for industrial and manufacturing projects.

  • Coordinate designs with other disciplines including architectural, mechanical, electrical, and process teams.

  • Assist in site layout, equipment foundation, and building structure design.

  • Review and update drawings based on engineering markups, redlines, and field changes.

  • Ensure all design work complies with applicable codes, standards, and project specifications.

What We’re Looking For

  • Structural design experience in an industrial or manufacturing environment.

  • Proficiency in Revit and AutoCAD required; experience with Navisworks, Plant 3D, ACC Docs, and SendTo preferred.

  • Knowledge of steel and concrete design standards and detailing practices.

  • Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced, project-driven setting.

  • Excellent communication and teamwork skills.

job.title: Program Controls Manager - Transmission & Distribution (34634)
job.description:
Program Controls Manager - Transmission & Distribution
Fort Worth, TX (On-site)

Benefits: Access to client benefits package 

Description: The Program Controls Manager is responsible for managing the Controls budget and schedule on various related projects within an over client program. 

Responsibilities:
  • Responsible for managing quality and consistency across all control's practices within the client's program and interface with the clients, attend program meetings, and create statistical reports for management on a recurring or as need basis.
  • Manage and present detailed schedule and earned value analysis and assess the impacts(s) of design/construction changes and schedule alterations, provide direction, and specialized assistance for the resolution of complex program schedule and cost issues.
  • Manage the development, implementation and maintenance of a management reporting data warehouse to present valuable program controls reports and data.
  • Lead and direct schedule management involves critical path method scheduling techniques, estimating, project cost management, forecasting and document control across a program.
  • Manage quality review of Earned Value Management system reporting.
  • Lead quality review and present cost management and forecast reporting across the program to stakeholders.
  • Implement and manage risk management process by identifying and mitigating risk potentials to the project.
  • Lead the Interactive Program Planning Meetings (IPPM).
  • Manage quality review and implementation of program execution manuals.
  • Analyze program forecast project costs including performance against an established schedule with critical milestones.
  • Manage and maintain effective relationships with existing and potential clients, customers and contractors to develop additional business.
  • Responsible for recruitment, development, training and retention and evaluating performance of the program controls team.
  • Performs other duties as assigned.
  • Complies with all policies and standards.
Qualifications:
  • Bachelor’s degree in engineering, Construction Management or related field.
  •  7 years direct project controls experience or managing industry specific programs in project controls required.
  • Applicable experience may be substituted for degree requirement.
  • Field experience in facilitating and producing detailed construction schedules and interfacing those construction schedules with engineering and procurement activities required.
  • Experience with Oracle Primavera P6 preferred.
  • Experience with Project Management Information System(s) preferred.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • Excellent interpersonal skills.
  • Proficient with Microsoft office.

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other legally protected status. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to any of these characteristics.

job.title: Air and Waste Program Manager (34520)
job.description:
Air & Waste Program Manager

Position Overview

The Air & Waste Program Manager provides strategic leadership, technical oversight, and operational management for air quality and waste management consulting programs. This role leads multidisciplinary project teams, manages client relationships, ensures regulatory compliance, and supports the growth of air and waste services while maintaining high standards of quality and client satisfaction.

This position is responsible for developing, implementing, and overseeing programs to ensure compliance with applicable air quality and waste regulations. The Program Manager conducts complex air quality assessments, manages permitting processes, analyzes emissions data, interfaces with regulatory agencies, and provides technical leadership and mentorship to staff while serving as a trusted advisor to clients.

Responsibilities

  • Lead and manage the Air & Waste program, including staff supervision, mentoring, and performance management

  • Provide senior-level technical guidance on air quality permitting, compliance, emissions reporting, and waste management programs

  • Review and approve technical deliverables such as permit applications, compliance reports, emissions inventories, air dispersion modeling analyses (e.g., AERMOD, CalEEMod, HARP), and air quality impact assessments

  • Align departmental objectives with broader organizational strategy in coordination with senior leadership

  • Develop and implement internal processes, standard operating procedures, and quality control measures

  • Oversee multiple projects simultaneously, ensuring scope, schedule, budget, and quality objectives are met

  • Serve as a primary point of contact for key clients and maintain long-term client relationships

  • Provide technical guidance and mentoring to junior staff and specialists

  • Review and approve waste management deliverables and advise clients on compliance with RCRA and other applicable federal, state, and local regulations

  • Stay current with evolving air quality and waste regulations, guidance, and modeling practices

  • Identify opportunities to expand services and support growth of the air and waste practice

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in environmental science, environmental engineering, chemical engineering, or a related field (Master’s preferred)

  • 8-10 years of progressive experience in air quality compliance, emissions monitoring, or environmental engineering

  • Strong knowledge of Clean Air Act regulations, New Source Review (NSR), Title V permitting, and emissions reporting
     
    Knowledge of AERMOD, CalEEMod, HARP2, and RMP

  • Experience with air dispersion modeling tools and emissions monitoring systems

  • Proven leadership and mentoring capabilities

  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and technical writing skills

  • Ability to effectively interface with regulatory agencies and industry stakeholders

  • Valid driver’s license and acceptable driving record

Preferred Qualifications

  • Professional Engineer (PE) or similar professional certification

  • Experience working directly with state and federal regulatory agencies

Travel Requirements

  • Occasional domestic overnight travel (approximately 10–20%) to support client engagements and project needs

  • Travel may be scheduled or short-notice and may involve job sites, field locations, or regional offices

  • Compliance with safety protocols and use of required PPE during site visits

Physical Demands & Work Environment

  • Hybrid work environment with responsibilities split between office and field settings

  • Office work includes extended computer use and regular in-person collaboration

job.title: Lead AWS Operations Engineer (W2 and Local to Chicago) (34565)
job.description:
Myticas's direct client, based in Chicago, IL, is currently seeking a Cloud Specialist (AWS) for a 1 Year On-site contract position.

NOTE: Looking for W2 candidates.

Top Required Hard Skills:

Subject matter expertise: Demonstrated advanced experience integrating legacy platforms and applications with AWS.
AIOps proficiency: Strong experience with AIOps, including the ability to provide guidance, leadership, and automation solutions.
Production monitoring: Extensive background in production monitoring using tools such as Grafana, Dynatrace, Splunk, and CloudWatch.

Top Required Soft Skills:

• Problem-solving ability: Advanced capability to solve problems by analyzing available information, prioritizing tasks, and making timely decisions. This includes correlating data using standard business and technology tools, spotting trends, and applying sound security and risk management principles.
• Time management and prioritization: Exceptional skills in assessing necessary activities and prioritizing assignments. This requires excellent time management, prioritization skills, and a strong work ethic.
• Presentation and communication: Proficiency in creating and delivering presentations to both technical and business audiences, with an understanding of the differences in communication strategies required for each.

Job Description:
As Cloud Specialist (AWS), you will serve as a lead technical operations expert responsible for the design, implementation, and support of cloud-based information technology solutions in both current and future states. In this role, you will be tasked with building, maintaining, and operationalizing infrastructure, security services, and system components in a government cloud AWS environment to deliver highly available and scalable services. You will join an on-call rotation and are expected to provide expertise and technical leadership on any issues that may arise.
Our team is actively engaged in projects to migrate legacy applications to AWS, innovate in AIOps, and enforce a strong baseline for operational readiness. This role demands confidence, leadership qualities, professionalism, and integrity.

job.title: Talent Acquisition Manager (34587)
job.description:
Talent Acquisition Manager
Location: Lake Charles, LA (On-site)

Benefits: Standard Medical, Dental, 401(k)
Hourly  Range: $40-$45/hr.

Summary:
The Talent Acquisition Manager, is responsible for overseeing and coordinating the full lifecycle of the staffing plan in support of the site. This role will work with key managers to determine staffing needs maintaining excellent relationships by providing a positive and professional experience. The Talent Acquisition Manager will utilize internal and external resources to support forecasting and recruiting needs including advertising, sourcing, identifying, developing candidate pools, screening, coordinating/leading interview teams and recommending candidates. 

Responsibilities:
  • Utilize staffing plan objectives to prioritize ‎and set goals‎, ‎planning and anticipating staffing needs and requirements‎.
  • Align company goals, strategies and tactics in identifying and securing top talent.
  • Assist managers with forecasting manpower needs, defining recruitment strategies, and coordinating logistics for open requisitions.
  • Add value beyond transactional tasks by pursuing opportunities to refine and improve process, identifying potential trends/issues.
  • Develop opportunities to create and maintain pipelines of candidates to meet short-term and longer-term needs.
  • Maintain expert knowledge of openings and provide proactive solutions to fill requisitions quickly and with high quality talent.
  • Lead the recruitment process for assigned positions, ensuring positive candidate experience.
  • Assist in the development and execution of sourcing strategies to include professional networking, direct sourcing, employee referrals and advertising.
  • Develop and manage recruiting metrics to accurately assess the effectiveness of the recruiting strategies.
  • Advance the overall recruitment strategy, seeking opportunities to improve sourcing channels, employment brand and sourcing effectiveness.
  • Perform supervisory responsibilities including evaluating staff performance ‎and encouraging staff development and training.
  • Interface with all ‎divisions and corporate support groups and overseeing compliance of the employment process‎.
  • Other duties within the Talent Acquisition department as assigned.
Qualifications:
  • A minimum of 4 years’ experience in recruiting within a large corporate environment or search firm with a focus on craft, engineering, construction, or related industries is strongly preferred.
  • Candidates should demonstrate proven success in identifying and attracting top talent using innovative, yet cost effective recruiting methodologies.
  • A high level of professionalism, including knowledge of human resources related regulations, is required, as is the ability to handle sensitive and confidential information appropriately.
  • Candidates should possess excellent project management, writing, oral, communication and presentation skills, as well as proficiency with the MS Office Suite.
  • A thorough understanding of advertising, networking, social media, and recruiting industry trends is essential for success.
  • Candidates must have the ability to manage multiple priorities, remaining flexible and adaptable. The individual selected must demonstrate strong initiative and solid business judgment abilities.
  • Experience in a construction, oil and gas, or engineering company is preferred.
  • An exceptional level of customer focus is required, coupled with strong interpersonal and teamwork skills.
  • Bachelor’s degree 

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other legally protected status. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to any of these characteristics.

job.title: Industrial Technician (34648)
job.description:
Industrial Technician
East Portal, CO (On-site)

Benefits: Standard Medial, Dental, 401(k)
Pay Range: $28.00 /hr. - $35.00 /hr.    

Summary: As an Industrial Tech, you’ll join a collaborative team ensuring that our clients can deliver their services – uninterrupted. Based at East Portal, CO you’ll have the opportunity to perform a variety of tasks in the operation, maintenance, calibration, diagnosis, and repair of the equipment, instruments, processes and support systems of an industrial wastewater treatment facility. 

Responsibilities:
  • Operate and monitor a single industrial wastewater treatment plant.
  • Work with ultra membrane filtration, centrifuge operation, sludge decanting/processing, pH/turbidity meters, motorized valves, generators, and more. 
  • Collect regular compliance samples, performing bench laboratory tests, overseeing contractors on site, and performing other duties as required to keep the treatment plant functioning properly. 
  • Take action to ensure the safety, health and well-being of your team.
Qualifications:
  • Experience with the operation and maintenance of industrial equipment.
  • Experience with ultra membrane filtration.
  • Knowledge of computer software including but not limited to Microsoft Word, Excel, Outlook, and data management software.
  • Experience working in water or wastewater treatment.
  • A valid driver’s license with no major infractions.

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other legally protected status. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to any of these characteristics.

job.title: Estimator (34631)
job.description:
Estimator
Wood Dale, IL (On-site- Direct Hire)

Salary Range: $80,000 – $95,000

Benefits: Casual and Fun Office Environment, Medical, Dental, Vision, 401(k), AD&D, Life insurance, PTO, Bonus Incentive program

Job Summary

The Estimator plays a key role in driving project success by delivering accurate, detailed cost estimates for custom fixtures, millwork, and accessories. Using a component‑driven system, you’ll collaborate closely with engineering, sales, and project management to develop cost‑effective, practical design solutions for both standard and highly customized applications.


This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a fast‑paced environment, enjoys solving complex manufacturing challenges, and brings exceptional time management, precision, and process‑driven thinking to every project.

Key Responsibilities
  • Prepare accurate and timely price estimates for custom fixtures, accessories, and millwork
  • Determine material and labor requirements based on customer specifications
  • Partner with engineering or project managers to recommend cost‑effective, manufacturable design solutions
  • Research and specify appropriate materials for each project
  • Solicit, review, and compare vendor and subcontractor quotes
  • Evaluate post‑production costs to ensure ongoing quoting accuracy
  • Manage multiple small projects simultaneously while meeting deadlines and maintaining high quality and accuracy
Qualifications
  • 5+ years of estimating experience, ideally in retail fixtures, millwork, or related manufacturing industries
  • Associate’s degree in Engineering, Design, Project Management, or related field (Bachelor’s preferred)
  • Background may be rooted in engineering or project management—both are welcomed
  • Strong knowledge of multi‑material fabrication, including wood, metal, plastics, and glass
  • Solid understanding of manufacturing processes such as metal fabrication and custom millwork
  • Experience with 3D solid modeling software (Autodesk Inventor preferred)
  • Strong numeracy, analytical skills, and proficiency with MS Office (Outlook, Excel, Word)
  • Familiarity with ERP systems required
  • Excellent problem‑solving abilities and a proven track record of meeting tight deadlines
  • Exceptional attention to detail, with the ability to prioritize and independently manage multiple projects

job.title: Piping Designer - AutoCAD Plant 3D (34582)
job.description:
Staff Mechanical Designer
Location: Charlotte. NC or Atlanta, GA (ONSITE)
Pay Range: $47.25 - $57.75 per hour
Benefits: Standard Medical, Dental, 401(k)

Job Description

Perform various phases of planning, site evaluations, analytical designs, report preparation, specifications, and preparation of proposal and construction documentation and mechanical plans as part of a project team. This position will work on various projects with a diverse portfolio of project types and clients.   

Responsibilities
  • Perform a variety of assignments using prescribed methods and standard techniques including drawing sequence and presentation, incorporating various system components, and refining rough sketches. 
  • Follow all company, client and project design and drafting standards.
  • Produce complete PFDs and P&IDs from verbal or written description from the design engineer.
  • Produce complete plot plans, sections, and details from verbal or written description from the design engineer. Isometric experience a plus.
  • Execute basic formulas, principles and practices, and use acquired knowledge of fundamental concepts and practices pertaining to the mechanical discipline.
  • Plan and develop drawings for production work including problem analysis, design layout and calculations on non-routine assignments of substantial variety and complexity.
  • Execute variety of mechanical design assignments using prescribed methods and standard techniques including drawing sequence and presentation, sizing various system components and refining rough sketches.
  • Execute changes to designs or sketches and redlines or mark-ups on ongoing projects.
  • Analyze reports, drawings, and tests to plan and design projects.
  • Interpret and incorporate design codes and specifications related to design requirements.
  • Review design drawings for quality assurance within scope, budget and schedule. 
  • Perform projects tasks and assignments within budgeted hours and deadlines.
  • Compute calculations for layouts, equipment, wiring diagrams, and schematics.
  • Responsible for the revisions of project drawings, plot files and project-related information are stored in a logical and well-structured manner.
  • Responsible for clear and concise written and verbal communications, as it relates to the project client coordination.
Requirements:
  • Bachelor Degree in drafting technology or related field and min 7 years related experience is Required OR Associate Degree  and min 8 years related experience is Required OR High School and min 9 years progressive detailing and design experience is Required.  
  • Advanced understanding of design software such as: AutoCAD, Plant3D, and Excel to develop the design deliverables.
  • Familiarity with MicroStation and CadWorx a plus.
  • Ability to learn and effectively utilize new design software programs.  
  • Advanced ability to utilize both 2D and 3D applications to prepare engineering drawings. 
  • Familiarity and skill with geolocated models, plant coordinate systems, and other spatial baselines a must.
  • Knowledge in design, calculations and design systems.  
  • Knowledge in the theory and practices of assigned discipline; competent in the fundamental concepts of other disciplines as they relate to the assigned discipline; and capable of operating related discipline design software necessary to execute production tasks.  
  • Ability to work collaboratively with others including, but not limited to Project Managers, Engineers and Designers in a multi-discipline and multi-office project team environment to resolve design or drawing issues as appropriate and to accomplish objectives.  
  • Excellent written & verbal communication skills.  
  • Strong analytical and problem solving skills, and attention to detail. 
  • Proven ability to manage multiple, competing projects and meet tight deadlines.

job.title: Structural Engineer (34646)
job.description:
Structural Engineer, PE
Location: Baton Rouge, Louisiana
Pay: $120k - $150k
Description: 

Who We Are

A growing engineering consultancy provides full-service engineering, procurement, and construction management (EPCm) services to industrial process manufacturers across multiple sectors including pulp & paper, chemical, consumer products, food & beverage, power, and heavy industrial markets nationwide. The organization is currently experiencing significant growth and offers an exciting opportunity to join a dynamic and expanding team.

What You’ll Be Doing

This individual will design structures capable of withstanding stresses and pressures resulting from human use and environmental conditions. Responsibilities include designing and configuring structures, overseeing drawing production, selecting appropriate foundation, building, and equipment support materials, inspecting construction work, and ensuring the structural integrity of foundations and facilities.

Additional responsibilities include:

  • Prepare reports, design criteria, cost estimates, designs, and drawings

  • Perform calculations related to pressures, loads, and stresses

  • Provide technical guidance on safe structural design and construction

  • Obtain planning and/or building regulation approvals

  • Coordinate with clients and multidisciplinary professionals including architects and engineers

  • Monitor and inspect contractor work to ensure structural compliance and soundness

  • Administer small subcontracts and manage structural project scopes

  • Review geotechnical reports to evaluate site conditions and foundation requirements

  • Utilize computer-aided design software for modeling and simulation

Ideal Background

The successful candidate will bring a proactive, solutions-oriented mindset and a strong ability to collaborate across disciplines. The role requires strong communication skills and the ability to manage multiple projects and client relationships simultaneously.

Qualifications include:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Civil or Structural Engineering

  • Licensed Professional Engineer (PE)

  • 3+ years of experience as a Structural Engineer

  • Experience in industrial structural engineering environments

  • Proficiency in STAAD, AutoCAD, and Revit

  • Experience with steel, concrete, and masonry design

  • Experience supporting heavy to light industrial projects

  • Familiarity with rotating equipment and vibration analysis

  • Advanced knowledge of construction methods, materials, and applicable regulations

job.title: Staff Level Piping Designer (CADWorx or Revit) (34597)
job.description:
Piping Design Technician 
Portland, OR (Hybrid)
Benefits: Standard Medical, Dental, 401(k)
Pay Range: $50.00 /hr. - $62.00 /hr.       

Summary: As a Career-Level Piping Designer, you’ll independently develop piping designs, potentially lead portions of project scope, and coordinate directly with engineers and other disciplines. You will provide technical input on constructability, model quality, and design standards while supporting workshare execution and contributing to estimating and change-management discussions. 

Responsibilities:
  • Develop complete piping layouts, routing, and model content in AutoCAD Plant 3D, Revit, or CADWorx with minimal direction.
  • Apply advanced piping design principles, including pipe sizing, routing optimization, and discipline coordination.
  • Lead equipment arrangement efforts and incorporate detailed vendor data into models and drawings.
  • Produce piping plans, sections, isometrics, and details in alignment with standards and project requirements.
  • Review and resolve multi discipline comments, clashes, and design issues using model-based coordination.
  • Identify scope changes, design impacts, and potential constructability concerns and communicate them proactively.
  • Support development of person-hour estimates, task planning, and design sequence for assigned work.
  • Coordinate with global workshare teams by providing clear task instructions, reviewing deliverables, and ensuring model consistency and quality.
  • Maintain adherence to design procedures, quality expectations, and established project workflows.

Qualifications: 
  • Associate’s degree in Design Technology or a related field (or equivalent practical experience of +2 years).
  • 5 + years of piping design experience in industrial, commercial, or manufacturing environments.
  • Proficiency in AutoCAD Plant 3D, Revit, or CADWorx for piping design and modeling.
  • Understanding of piping specifications, materials, equipment interfaces, and layout fundamentals.
  • Ability to read and interpret P&IDs, vendor drawings, and engineering documentation.
  • Demonstrated ability to work independently with general oversight while managing your assigned design tasks.
  • Strong communication and coordination skills within a multi discipline team environment.
  • Excellent organizational skills with a focus on model and drawing accuracy.



We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other legally protected status. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to any of these characteristics.

job.title: Associate Fulfillment Specialist (34656)
job.description:
Myticas's direct client, based in Wilmington, DE is currently seeking a Associate Fulfillment Specialist for a On-site contract position.

Pay Rate: $22-23/hr on W2.
Shifts Available: 12:00pm-9:00pm or Shift: 3:00pm-midnight (Mon-Thu), 1:30pm-10:30pm (Fri)

NOTE:  W2 Only!

Job Description:
As a Fulfillment Associate, you will help impact the lives of millions of people and shape society for the future. You will work in unison with our service teams to assist our clients remain in legal compliance. We are looking for individuals who are positive, energetic, accountable, flexible, and committed to self-development.

Basic Responsibilities:
The Fulfillment Associate for clients CT has primary responsibility for providing superior service by:
• Fulfilling orders for internal and external customers
• Researching and analyzing legal documents
• Submitting legal documents to the Secretary of State, Division of Corporations
• Retrieving legal documents from the Secretary of State, Division of Corporations
• Researching entity status
• Resolving or escalating issues
• Following requests through to completion
• Communicating progress to customers and internal teams
• Navigating quickly between multiple processing systems and databases quickly and accurately
• Able to work in a fast-paced, high-energy environment.
• Overtime and minimal (5%) travel may be required.
• Contributes to customer satisfaction and organizational success
• Participates in process improvement programs, best practices, and occasional special projects

Required Experience & Qualifications:
Preferred: Bachelor’s degree in Liberal Arts (e.g., Business, Communications, Marketing, or related discipline); OR, if no degree, 4 years of customer service experience.
• 4 years customer service experience, including:
• Integrating information from multiple sources quickly and accurately.
• Utilizing keen attention to detail skill accurately
• Utilizing communication skills, including both excellent verbal skills and written communication.
• Working independently while still being an integral part of a team.
• Knowledge of Microsoft Office / Adobe, ability to learn new applications quickly and effectively.
• Excellent analytical, organizational, time management and problem-solving skills.

job.title: Lab Tech - Micro (Laboratory Testing) (34657)
job.description:
Lab Tech-Micro - Laboratory Testing
Contract: W2– Candidates must be authorized to work in the U.S. on a W2 basis. 
Duration: 6 months with possible multi-year extension/s
Pay: $20-22 per hour on W2 
Hours:  Monday-Friday, 40hrs, 8-5pm, On-Site
Location: Grand Rapids, MI; Considering local candidates only

Micro Lab Technician: Laboratory Testing
This is an entry level position for a Microbiology Lab Technician, which operates under the Quality Control Unit. In this role you will be supporting testing and release of raw materials, finished consumer goods, and/or environmental samples.

This first shift position will work as part of a cohesive laboratory team which often uses a rotational schedule to keep the work interesting and to ensure workload is covered. You will be trained on multiple techniques within the micro laboratory and will be kept up-to-date with team meetings. Daily duties may include cleaning, record keeping, media preparation, and calibration of equipment. Once trained, occasional weekend work may be required.

Skills to be successful in the role:
• Flexible team player
• Attention to detail
• Ability to communicate and elevate issues to leadership
• Ability to sit, stand, and lift up to 50lbs
• GMP microbiology laboratory experience preferred

Required qualifications:
• Laboratory experience (in education or industry)
• Demonstrated ability to follow SOPs/test methods

Education: 2 years of college in a science related field and/or demonstrated experience in microbiology laboratory procedures

job.title: Senior UI/UX Designer (34658)
job.description:
Clover Consulting has a direct client in Grand Rapids, MI in need of a Senior UI/UX Designer for a 6+ months contract.  This is an On-Site role.  The job details are below.  Qualified candidates, please provide resume.  Thank you!

Senior UI/UX Designer
Contract: W2– Candidates must be authorized to work in the U.S. on a W2 basis. 
Duration: 6 months with possible multi-year extension/s
Pay: $55-60/hr  
Hours:  Monday-Friday, 40hrs.8-5pm, On-Site
Location: Grand Rapids, MI; Considering local candidates only

As a Senior Designer, you will turn ambiguous problems into shipped, measurable outcomes.  You lead the design for a product or pod, bridging the gap between user needs, business strategy and engineering feasibility.  You raise the bar for the entire design organization.  

RESPONSIBILITIES:
Strategic Discovery:  Lead discovery to define problems and user needs.  Translate insights into clear product opportunities and testable hypotheses.  

End-to-End Execution:  Design high-quality accessible interfaces (Web/iOS/Android) and user flows that move the needle on KPI's like activation and task success. 

AI-Augmented Workflow:  Leverage generative AI tools to accelerate ideation, automate repetitive tasks and synthesize large-scale research data.

System Ownership:  Evolve our design system (tokens, components, patterns); drive adoption and governance across platforms with "accessibility by default."

Technical Collaboration: Partner deeply with Engineering and PM's to refine requirements, navigate technical constraints and ensure high-quality Design QA. 

Leadership & Mentorship:  Elevate team standards through critiques and documented decision-making; mentor med/junior designers in craft and strategy.  

REQUIRED SKILLS:
Product Thinking:  Ability to link design decisions directly to business models and success metrics (CSAT, North Star metrics). 

Expert-level proficiency in Figma, including Auto Layout 6.0, Multi-mode Variables (for theming, localization, and accessibility), and logic-driven prototyping using conditional variables and expressions to simulate production-ready app behavior. 

AI Literacy:  Proficiency in AI-driven design tools (Figma, AI/Make) for rapid wireframing and UI exploration. 

Research Synthesis:  Ability to use AI tools (e.g. Co-Pilot) to distill user interviews and feedback into actionable themes. 

Universal Design:  Deep knowledge of WCAG 2.2 standards and ethical design patterns. 

Communicate strong evidence-based points of view, seek out critique and feedback to strengthen designs.

job.title: Data Scientist - AI, Analytics & Machine Learning (34638)
job.description:
We are seeking a highly motivated Data Scientist to join our analytics and AI team focused on driving data-driven decision-making across large-scale systems and telecommunications-related use cases. You will work on advanced data analytics, machine learning modeling, and statistical analysis to extract actionable insights, build predictive systems, and support strategic business and product decisions.

Key Responsibilities

  • Analyze large, heterogeneous datasets to identify patterns, trends, and insights that support business and product strategy.

  • Design, develop, and deploy machine learning and statistical models to solve real-world problems (predictive analytics, classification, regression, clustering).

  • Work closely with product managers, data engineers, and cross-functional teams to translate business requirements into scalable data science solutions.

  • Perform exploratory data analysis, feature engineering, and model evaluation.

  • Build data pipelines and integrate models into production systems where relevant.

  • Communicate insights and results clearly to technical and non-technical stakeholders through visualizations and reports.

  • Stay current with emerging techniques in machine learning, artificial intelligence, and data science best practices.

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Engineering, or a related discipline.

  • Strong programming skills in Python and experience with data science libraries (e.g., pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn).

  • Experience with machine learning modeling techniques, including supervised and unsupervised methods.

  • Solid statistical analysis skills and understanding of modeling assumptions and performance evaluation.

  • Experience working with large datasets and data engineering tools (e.g., SQL, Spark).

  • Ability to produce meaningful visualizations and presentations of analytical results.

  • Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work collaboratively in cross-functional teams.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with big data technologies and distributed computing frameworks (e.g., Spark, Hadoop).

  • Familiarity with cloud-based data platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP).

  • Knowledge of deep learning frameworks (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch) or experience with natural language processing.

  • Exposure to AI/ML model deployment workflows, MLOps tools, and CI/CD pipelines.

  • Experience with real-time streaming or event-driven analytics (e.g., Kafka).

job.title: Banking Solution Architect (Temenos R13) (34624)
job.description:
Our Financial client in the Toronto area, is seeking an experienced Banking Solution Architect for a long term contract to support a Lean Core Transformation Program. This role will provide targeted architectural and technical leadership, focused on Temenos platform assessment, rationalization, and modernization initiatives.

Key Responsibilities

  • Strong functional knowledge across Deposits, Lending, Customer, and Channel modules

  • Minimum 3 end-to-end core banking conversions/implementations in Architect or Lead Developer capacity

  • Executive presence and strong communication skills

job.title: Senior SAP Payroll Analyst (34630)
job.description:
Our client is seeking a Senior SAP Payroll Analyst for a long term remote contract to provide technical and production support for legislative and mandatory payroll requirements. The SAP Payroll module supports approximately 23,000 employees across multiple unions.

 

Key Responsibilities

  • Configure, build, and test SAP Payroll (ECC 6.0) for legislative/mandatory requirements

  • Provide production support across SAP Payroll and related modules

  • Configure payroll components including pay journals, pay rules, and schemas

  • Support end-to-end payroll processing including integration points and GL postings

  • Apply and test SAP Support Packs, including year-end payroll updates

  • Configure and support Time Management rules and schemas (work schedules, absences, attendances, quotas)

  • Support production and balancing of T4 and T4A

  • Work within a multi-union environment

Required Experience

  • 10+ years SAP production support experience

  • 10+ years SAP ECC 6.0 experience

  • 10+ years SAP Payroll configuration experience

  • 10+ years SAP Time Management configuration experience

  • 5+ years applying/testing SAP Support Packs (including year-end payroll changes)

  • 5+ years working in a multi-union environment

Nice to Have

  • Experience supporting Fiori Pay and Time applications

  • Experience supporting Workforce Solutions

job.title: Senior EBX Developer (Tibco EBX / MDM (34629)
job.description:
